main_claims.claim: Observation of collective structural quantum fluctuations in an 11-atom molecule (2-iodopyridine) in its ground state.
main_claims.notes: The claim is supported by coincident detection of atomic fragments from individual molecules undergoing Coulomb explosion, revealing correlated variations of ion momenta.

main_claims.claim: A general analytical approach for extracting information on high-dimensional structural dynamics using Coulomb explosion from partial momentum space coverage.
main_claims.notes: The paper establishes a scheme to analyze the variations in ion momenta covering a 33-dimensional space.

novelty_assessment: The methodology is highly novel, advancing the capacity to image ground-state quantum fluctuations from previously limited simple molecules (like triatomics) to complex, multi-atom polyatomic molecules.
score_rationale: The paper is a significant experimental achievement in chemical physics, directly observing collective quantum fluctuations in a complex molecule. The experimental technique (XFEL-induced Coulomb explosion) is robust and the results are field-advancing, justifying strong scores across all dimensions.
summary: The paper reports the direct observation of collective quantum fluctuations in the ground-state structure of a complex polyatomic molecule (2-iodopyridine). The authors achieve this by utilizing x-ray-induced Coulomb explosion imaging at an x-ray free-electron laser, demonstrating that structural fluctuations manifest as correlated variations in the momenta of the resulting ionic fragments. The methodology allows for the extraction of high-dimensional structural dynamics information even when covering only a fraction of the full momentum space.
